member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat
member.summary: Specifies the data format of a LSL channel.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Float32
member.summary: Used for up to 24-bit precision measurements in the appropriate physical unit (e.g. microvolts). Integers from -16777216 to 16777216 are represented accurately.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Double64
member.summary: Used for universal numeric data as long as network and disk constraints permit. The largest representable integer is 53-bit.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.String
member.summary: Used for variable-length ASCII strings or data blobs, such as video frames, complex event descriptions, etc.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Int32
member.summary: Used for high sampling rate digitized formats that require 32-bit precision. Depends critically on meta-data to represent meaningful units. Also useful for application event codes or other coded data.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Int16
member.summary: Used for very high sampling rate signals (40kHz+) or consumer-grade audio.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Int8
member.summary: Used for binary signals or other coded data. Not recommended for encoding string data.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Int64
member.summary: For future compatibility only. Support for this type is not yet exposed in all languages. Some clients will not be able to send or receive data of this type.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ChannelFormat.Undefined
member.summary: Specifies that the data can not be transmitted.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSLObject
member.summary:
C# API for the lab streaming layer.
The lab streaming layer provides a set of functions to make instrument data accessible in real time within a lab network. From there, streams can be picked up by recording programs, viewing programs or custom experiment applications that access data streams in real time.
The API covers two areas: - The "push API" allows to create stream outlets and to push data (regular or irregular measurement time series, event data, coded audio/video frames, etc.) into them. - The "pull API" allows to create stream inlets and read time-synched experiment data from them (for recording, viewing or experiment control).

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSLObject.DestroyLSLObject(System.IntPtr)
member.summary: To be implemented in inheriting classes: the liblsl function to destroy the internal object

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSL
member.summary: Static functions implemented in liblsl

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSL.DisposeArray(EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.StreamInfo[])
member.summary: Helper method to dispose a StreamInfo[] array
member.param: Array to be disposed

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSL.IRREGULAR_RATE
member.summary: Constant to indicate that a stream has variable sampling rate.

members.member: Constant to indicate that a sample has the next successive time stamp. This is an optional optimization to transmit less data per sample. The stamp is then deduced from the preceding one according to the stream's sampling rate (in the case of an irregular rate, the same time stamp as before will is assumed).

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSL.FOREVER
member.summary: A very large time duration (> 1 year) for timeout values. Note that significantly larger numbers can cause the timeout to be invalid on some operating systems (e.g., 32-bit UNIX).

members.member: Obtain a local system time stamp in seconds. The resolution is better than a millisecond. This reading can be used to assign time stamps to samples as they are being acquired. If the "age" of a sample is known at a particular time (e.g., from USB transmission delays), it can be used as an offset to local_clock() to obtain a better estimate of when a sample was actually captured. See stream_outlet::push_sample() for a use case.

Resolve all streams on the network. This function returns all currently available streams from any outlet on the network. The network is usually the subnet specified at the local router, but may also include a multicast group of machines (given that the network supports it), or list of hostnames. These details may optionally be customized by the experimenter in a configuration file (see Network Connectivity in the LSL wiki). This is the default mechanism used by the browsing programs and the recording program. @param wait_time The waiting time for the operation, in seconds, to search for streams. Warning: If this is too short (less than 0.5s) only a subset (or none) of the outlets that are present on the network may be returned. @return An array of stream info objects (excluding their desc field), any of which can subsequently be used to open an inlet. The full info can be retrieve from the inlet.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.LSL.check_error(System.Int32)
member.summary: Check an error condition and throw an exception if appropriate.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Native.channel_format_t
member.summary: Data format of a channel (each transmitted sample holds an array of channels).

members.member: Post-processing options for stream inlets.

Name of the stream. This is a human-readable name. For streams offered by device modules, it refers to the type of device or product series that is generating the data of the stream. If the source is an application, the name may be a more generic or specific identifier. Multiple streams with the same name can coexist, though potentially at the cost of ambiguity (for the recording app or experimenter).

Content type of the stream. The content type is a short string such as "EEG", "Gaze" which describes the content carried by the channel (if known). If a stream contains mixed content this value need not be assigned but may instead be stored in the description of channel types. To be useful to applications and automated processing systems using the recommended content types is preferred. Content types usually follow those pre-defined in https://github.com/sccn/xdf/wiki/Meta-Data (or web search for: XDF meta-data).
Number of channels of the stream. A stream has at least one channel; the channel count stays constant for all samples.

Sampling rate of the stream, according to the source (in Hz). If a stream is irregularly sampled, this should be set to IRREGULAR_RATE.
Note that no data will be lost even if this sampling rate is incorrect or if a device has temporary hiccups, since all samples will be recorded anyway (except for those dropped by the device itself). However, when the recording is imported into an application, a good importer may correct such errors more accurately if the advertised sampling rate was close to the specs of the device.
Channel format of the stream. All channels in a stream have the same format. However, a device might offer multiple time-synched streams each with its own format.

Unique identifier of the stream's source, if available. The unique source (or device) identifier is an optional piece of information that, if available, allows that endpoints (such as the recording program) can re-acquire a stream automatically once it is back online.
Protocol version used to deliver the stream.
Creation time stamp of the stream. This is the time stamp when the stream was first created (as determined via local_clock() on the providing machine).

Unique ID of the stream outlet instance (once assigned). This is a unique identifier of the stream outlet, and is guaranteed to be different across multiple instantiations of the same outlet (e.g., after a re-start).

Session ID for the given stream. The session id is an optional human-assigned identifier of the recording session. While it is rarely used, it can be used to prevent concurrent recording activitites on the same sub-network (e.g., in multiple experiment areas) from seeing each other's streams (assigned via a configuration file by the experimenter, see Network Connectivity in the LSL wiki).
Hostname of the providing machine.

Extended description of the stream. It is highly recommended that at least the channel labels are described here. See code examples on the LSL wiki. Other information, such as amplifier settings, measurement units if deviating from defaults, setup information, subject information, etc., can be specified here, as well. Meta-data recommendations follow the XDF file format project (github.com/sccn/xdf/wiki/Meta-Data or web search for: XDF meta-data).
Important: if you use a stream content type for which meta-data recommendations exist, please try to lay out your meta-data in agreement with these recommendations for compatibility with other applications.
A stream outlet. Outlets are used to make streaming data (and the meta-data) available on the lab network.
Check whether consumers are currently registered. While it does not hurt, there is technically no reason to push samples if there is no consumer.
Wait until some consumer shows up (without wasting resources). @return True if the wait was successful, false if the timeout expired.
Retrieve the stream info provided by this outlet. This is what was used to create the stream (and also has the Additional Network Information fields assigned).
A stream inlet. Inlets are used to receive streaming data (and meta-data) from the lab network.

* Construct a new stream inlet from a resolved stream info. * @param info A resolved stream info object (as coming from one of the resolver functions). * Note: the stream_inlet may also be constructed with a fully-specified stream_info, * if the desired channel format and count is already known up-front, but this is * strongly discouraged and should only ever be done if there is no time to resolve the * stream up-front (e.g., due to limitations in the client program). * @param max_buflen Optionally the maximum amount of data to buffer (in seconds if there is a nominal * sampling rate, otherwise x100 in samples). Recording applications want to use a fairly * large buffer size here, while real-time applications would only buffer as much as * they need to perform their next calculation. * @param max_chunklen Optionally the maximum size, in samples, at which chunks are transmitted * (the default corresponds to the chunk sizes used by the sender). * Recording applications can use a generous size here (leaving it to the network how * to pack things), while real-time applications may want a finer (perhaps 1-sample) granularity. If left unspecified (=0), the sender determines the chunk granularity. * @param recover Try to silently recover lost streams that are recoverable (=those that that have a source_id set). * In all other cases (recover is false or the stream is not recoverable) functions may throw a * LostException if the stream's source is lost (e.g., due to an app or computer crash).

Drop the current data stream. All samples that are still buffered or in flight will be dropped and transmission and buffering of data for this inlet will be stopped. If an application stops being interested in data from a source (temporarily or not) but keeps the outlet alive, it should call close_stream() to not waste unnecessary system and network resources.

Query whether samples are currently available for immediate pickup. Note that it is not a good idea to use samples_available() to determine whether a pull_*() call would block: to be sure, set the pull timeout to 0.0 or an acceptably low value. If the underlying implementation supports it, the value will be the number of samples available (otherwise it will be 1 or 0).

Query whether the clock was potentially reset since the last call to was_clock_reset(). This is a rarely-used function that is only useful to applications that combine multiple time_correction values to estimate precise clock drift; it allows to tolerate cases where the source machine was hot-swapped or restarted in between two measurements.

A lightweight XML element tree; models the .desc() field of stream_info. Has a name and can have multiple named children or have text content as value; attributes are omitted. Insider note: The interface is modeled after a subset of pugixml's node type and is compatible with it. See also http://pugixml.googlecode.com/svn/tags/latest/docs/manual/access.html for additional documentation.
Get the first child of the element.
Get the last child of the element.
Get the next sibling in the children list of the parent node.
Get the previous sibling in the children list of the parent node.
Get the parent node.
Get a child with a specified name.
Get the next sibling with the specified name.
Get the previous sibling with the specified name.
Whether this node is empty.
Whether this is a text body (instead of an XML element). True both for plain char data and CData.
Name of the element.
Value of the element.
Get child value (value of the first child that is text).
Get child value of a child with a specified name.
Append a child node with a given name, which has a (nameless) plain-text child with the given text value.
Prepend a child node with a given name, which has a (nameless) plain-text child with the given text value.
Set the text value of the (nameless) plain-text child of a named child node.
Set the element's name. @return False if the node is empty.
Set the element's value. @return False if the node is empty.
Append a child element with the specified name.
Prepend a child element with the specified name.
Append a copy of the specified element as a child.
Prepend a child element with the specified name.
Remove a child element with the specified name.
Remove a specified child element.
A convenience class that resolves streams continuously in the background throughout its lifetime and which can be queried at any time for the set of streams that are currently visible on the network.

Construct a new continuous_resolver that resolves all streams on the network. This is analogous to the functionality offered by the free function resolve_streams(). @param forget_after When a stream is no longer visible on the network (e.g., because it was shut down), this is the time in seconds after which it is no longer reported by the resolver.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.LostException
member.summary: Represents an error indicating that the source of a stream inlet has been irrecoverably lost.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.InternalException
member.summary: Represents an error indicating an internal failure has occurred inside liblsl.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet
member.summary: Represents an operator that pulls data buffers from the specified LSL stream into an observable sequence.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the LSL stream from which to pull data. If no name is specified, the first stream with the specified content type will be used.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet.ContentType
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the content type of the LSL stream. If no content type is specified, the first stream of any type with the specified name will be used.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet.ChannelCount
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the number of channels in the LSL stream.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet.ChunkSize
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the number of samples in each chunk. If no value is specified, the output will be a single sample for each channel.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet.ChannelFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the data format of each channel in the LSL stream.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamInlet.ProcessingOptions
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the postprocessing time synchronization options to use on the LSL stream.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions
member.summary: Specifies options for post-processing of samples for an LSL stream inlet.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions.None
member.summary: No automatic post-processing. Provides ground truth timestamps for manual post-processing.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions.Clocksync
member.summary: Perform automatic clock synchronization.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions.Dejitter
member.summary: Remove random jitter from timestamps using a trend-adjusted smoothing algorithm.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions.Monotonize
member.summary: Force timestamps to be monotonically ascending.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions.Threadsafe
member.summary: Post-processing is thread-safe (same inlet can be read from by multiple threads) at the cost of more CPU usage.

member.name: F:EmotionalCities.Lsl.ProcessingOptions.All
member.summary: Use all available postprocessing options.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet
member.summary: Represents an operator that pushes data buffers from an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the LSL stream to which data will be pushed.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.ContentType
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the content type of the LSL stream. If no content type is specified, the simple name of the input type will be used.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.ChannelCount
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the number of channels in the LSL stream.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Selector
member.summary: Gets or sets the inner properties that will be selected when writing each element of the sequence.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.SampleRate
member.summary: Gets or sets the nominal sampling rate used by the data source, in Hz. If a value of zero is specified, the data source will be considered to have irregular timing.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.SourceId
member.summary: Gets or sets the unique identifier of the stream data source.
member.remarks: The unique source (or device) identifier is an optional piece of information that, if available, allows endpoints to re-acquire a stream automatically once it is back online.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Byte})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of unsigned 8-bit integers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of unsigned 8-bit integers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Byte[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of unsigned 8-bit samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of unsigned 8-bit samples to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Byte[0:,0:]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of unsigned 8-bit chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of unsigned 8-bit chunks to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of columns as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{System.Byte}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped unsigned 8-bit integers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped unsigned 8-bit integers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample{System.Byte}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped unsigned 8-bit samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped unsigned 8-bit samples to push to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk{System.Byte}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped unsigned 8-bit chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped unsigned 8-bit chunks to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Byte})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each element into an unsigned 8-bit integer.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Byte[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each element into unsigned 8-bit samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{``0}},System.Func{``0,System.Byte})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into an unsigned 8-bit integer.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{``0}},System.Func{``0,System.Byte[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into unsigned 8-bit samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Int16})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of signed 16-bit integers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of signed 16-bit integers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Int16[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of signed 16-bit samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of signed 16-bit samples to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Int16[0:,0:]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of signed 16-bit chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of signed 16-bit chunks to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of columns as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{System.Int16}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped signed 16-bit integers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped signed 16-bit integers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample{System.Int16}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped signed 16-bit samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped signed 16-bit samples to push to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk{System.Int16}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped signed 16-bit chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped signed 16-bit chunks to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Int16})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each element into a signed 16-bit integer.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Int16[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each element into signed 16-bit samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{``0}},System.Func{``0,System.Int16})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into a signed 16-bit integer.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{``0}},System.Func{``0,System.Int16[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into signed 16-bit samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of signed 32-bit integers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of signed 32-bit integers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Int32[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of signed 32-bit samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of signed 32-bit samples to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Int32[0:,0:]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of signed 32-bit chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of signed 32-bit chunks to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of columns as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{System.Int32}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped signed 32-bit integers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped signed 32-bit integers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample{System.Int32}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped signed 32-bit samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped signed 32-bit samples to push to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk{System.Int32}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped signed 32-bit chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped signed 32-bit chunks to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Int32})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each element into a signed 32-bit integer.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0},System.Func{``0,System.Int32[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each element into signed 32-bit samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{``0}},System.Func{``0,System.Int32})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into a signed 32-bit integer.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into signed 32-bit samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Single})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of 32-bit floating-point values into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of 32-bit floating-point numbers to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Single[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of 32-bit floating-point samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of 32-bit floating-point samples to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.Single[0:,0:]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of 32-bit floating-point chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of 32-bit floating-point chunks to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of columns as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the chunks to the LSL stream.

member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped 32-bit floating-point values into the specified LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.String})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of variable length strings into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of variable length strings to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.String[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of variable length samples into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of variable length samples to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{System.String[0:,0:]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of variable length chunks into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of variable length chunks to push to the LSL stream. Each array must have the same number of columns as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the chunks to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{System.String}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped variable length strings into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped variable length strings to push to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped variable length samples into the specified LSL stream.

member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the values to the LSL stream.

member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the samples to the LSL stream.

member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into a variable length string.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which values to write to the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped values to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process``1(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{``0}},System.Func{``0,System.String[]})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence into the specified LSL stream, using a selector function to transform each timestamped element into variable length samples.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped elements to push to the LSL stream.
The transform function used to select which samples to write to the LSL stream. Each sample array must have the same number of values as the total number of channels in the LSL stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped samples to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{OpenCV.Net.Mat})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of multi-channel buffers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of multi-channel buffers to push to the LSL stream. Each buffer must have the same number of rows as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the buffers to the LSL stream.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.StreamOutlet.Process(System.IObservable{EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{OpenCV.Net.Mat}})
member.summary: Pushes an observable sequence of timestamped multi-channel buffers into the specified LSL stream.
member.param: The sequence of timestamped multi-channel buffers to push to the LSL stream. Each buffer must have the same number of rows as the total number of channels in the stream.
member.returns: An observable sequence that is identical to the source sequence but where there is an additional side effect of writing the timestamped multi-channel buffers to the LSL stream.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamp
member.summary: Represents an operator that records the local LSL timestamp for each element produced by an observable sequence.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamp.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0})
member.summary: Records the local LSL timestamp for each element produced by an observable sequence.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The source sequence to timestamp elements for.
member.returns: An observable sequence containing recorded LSL timestamp information for each element in the sequence.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamp.Process``1(System.IObservable{``0[]})
member.summary: Records the local LSL timestamp for each sample produced by an observable sequence.
member.typeparam: The type of the elements in the sequence.
member.param: The source sequence to timestamp samples for.
member.returns: An observable sequence containing recorded LSL timestamp information for each sample in the sequence.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1
member.summary: Represents a timestamped value.
member.typeparam: The type of the value.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.#ctor(`0,System.Double)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified value and timestamp.
member.param: The value.
The timestamp, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.Timestamp
member.summary: Gets the timestamp, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.Value
member.summary: Gets the value.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.Deconstruct(`0@,System.Double@)
member.summary: Deconstructs the components of a timestamped value into separate variables.
member.param: The value.
The timestamp, in fractional seconds.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.Equals(EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{`0})
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this instance has the same value and timestamp as a specified structure.
member.param: The structure to compare to this instance.
member.returns: true if has the same value and timestamp as this instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Tests to see whether the specified object is an structure with the same value and timestamp as this structure.
member.param: The to test.
member.returns: true if is an and has the same value and timestamp as this ; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns a hash code for this structure.
member.returns: An integer value that specifies a hash value for this structure.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.ToString
member.summary: Creates a representation of this structure.
member.returns: A containing the and properties of this structure.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.op_Equality(EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{`0},EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{`0})
member.summary: Tests whether two structures are equal.
member.param: The structure on the left of the equality operator.
The structure on the right of the equality operator.
member.returns: true if and have equal value and timestamp; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped`1.op_Inequality(EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{`0},EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped{`0})
member.summary: Tests whether two structures are different.
member.param: The structure on the left of the inequality operator.
The structure on the right of the inequality operator.
member.returns: true if and differ either in value or timestamp; false if and are equal.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped
member.summary: Provides static methods for creating timestamped payload objects.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.Timestamped.Create``1(``0,System.Double)
member.summary: Creates a new timestamped value.
member.typeparam: The type of the value.
member.param: The value.
The timestamp, in fractional seconds.
member.returns: A new instance of the class with the specified value and timestamp.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1
member.summary: Represents a timestamped chunk.
member.typeparam: The type of the data samples.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.#ctor(`0[0:,0:],System.Double[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the structure with the specified data samples and corresponding timestamps.
member.param: The data samples of the timestamped chunk.
The timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.Timestamps
member.summary: Gets the timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.Data
member.summary: Gets the data samples of the timestamped chunk.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.Deconstruct(`0[0:,0:]@,System.Double[]@)
member.summary: Deconstructs the components of a timestamped chunk into separate variables.
member.param: The data samples of the timestamped chunk.
The timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.Equals(EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk{`0})
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this instance has the same data and timestamps as a specified structure.
member.param: The structure to compare to this instance.
member.returns: true if has the same data and timestamps as this instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Tests to see whether the specified object is a structure with the same data and timestamps as this structure.
member.param: The to test.
member.returns: true if is a and has the same data and timestamps as this ; otherwise, false.

member.summary: Returns a hash code for this structure.
member.returns: An integer value that specifies a hash value for this structure.

member.summary: Creates a representation of this structure.
member.returns: A containing the and properties of this structure.

member.summary: Tests whether two structures are equal.
member.param: The structure on the left of the equality operator.
The structure on the right of the equality operator.
member.returns: true if and have the same data and timestamps; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk`1.op_Inequality(EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk{`0},EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk{`0})
member.summary: Tests whether two structures are different.
member.param: The structure on the left of the inequality operator.
The structure on the right of the inequality operator.
member.returns: true if and differ either in data or timestamps; false if and are equal.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer
member.summary: Represents a timestamped chunk buffer.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.#ctor(OpenCV.Net.Mat,OpenCV.Net.Mat)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the structure with the specified data samples and corresponding timestamps.
member.param: The data samples of the timestamped chunk buffer.
The timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.Timestamps
member.summary: Gets the timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.Data
member.summary: Gets the data samples of the timestamped chunk buffer.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.Deconstruct(OpenCV.Net.Mat@,OpenCV.Net.Mat@)
member.summary: Deconstructs the components of a timestamped chunk buffer into separate variables.
member.param: The data samples of the timestamped chunk buffer.
The timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.Equals(EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer)
member.summary: Returns a value indicating whether this instance has the same data and timestamps as a specified structure.
member.param: The structure to compare to this instance.
member.returns: true if has the same data and timestamps as this instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.Equals(System.Object)
member.summary: Tests to see whether the specified object is a structure with the same data and timestamps as this structure.
member.param: The to test.
member.returns: true if is a and has the same data and timestamps as this ; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.GetHashCode
member.summary: Returns a hash code for this structure.
member.returns: An integer value that specifies a hash value for this structure.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.ToString
member.summary: Creates a representation of this structure.
member.returns: A containing the and properties of this structure.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.op_Equality(EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer,EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer)
member.summary: Tests whether two structures are equal.
member.param: The structure on the left of the equality operator.
The structure on the right of the equality operator.
member.returns: true if and have the same data and timestamps; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer.op_Inequality(EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer,EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunkBuffer)
member.summary: Tests whether two structures are different.
member.param: The structure on the left of the inequality operator.
The structure on the right of the inequality operator.
member.returns: true if and differ either in data or timestamps; false if and are equal.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk
member.summary: Provides static methods for creating timestamped chunk objects.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk.Create``1(``0[0:,0:],System.Double[])
member.summary: Creates a new timestamped chunk value.
member.typeparam: The type of the data samples in the timestamped payload.
member.param: The data samples of the timestamped chunk.
The timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.
member.returns: A new instance of the class with the specified array of samples and corresponding timestamps.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedChunk.Create(OpenCV.Net.Mat,OpenCV.Net.Mat)
member.summary: Creates a new timestamped chunk buffer.
member.param: The data samples of the timestamped chunk buffer.
The timestamps for each data sample, in fractional seconds.
member.returns: A new instance of the class with the specified array of samples and corresponding timestamps.

member.name: T:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample`1
member.summary: Represents a timestamped sample.
member.typeparam: The type of the data samples.

member.name: M:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample`1.#ctor(`0[],System.Double)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the structure with the specified data samples and corresponding timestamp.
member.param: The sample values for each channel of the timestamped sample.
The timestamp of the data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample`1.Timestamp
member.summary: Gets the timestamp of the data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.name: P:EmotionalCities.Lsl.TimestampedSample`1.Data
member.summary: Gets the sample values for each channel of the timestamped sample.

member.summary: Deconstructs the components of a timestamped sample into separate variables.
member.param: The sample values for each channel of the timestamped sample.
The timestamp of the data sample, in fractional seconds.

member.returns: true if has the same data and timestamp as this instance; otherwise, false.

member.summary: Tests to see whether the specified object is a structure with the same data and timestamp as this structure.

member.returns: true if is a and has the same data and timestamp as this ; otherwise, false.

member.summary: Provides static methods for creating timestamped sample objects.
